title: What is a Cost Function?
concept: A cost function measures a model's overall prediction error across a dataset, giving a single score we want to minimize.

exercises.prompt: Recap: In the ice cream sales scenario, why did a linear regression model perform poorly when the trend was curved?

options.text: It violated the linearity assumption, as a straight line cannot fit a curved pattern
Linear regression cannot handle temperature data
Linear regression only works on datasets with millions of rows

exercises.explain: Right — the linearity assumption requires a straight-line relationship. If the actual pattern is curved, a linear model will struggle.

cards.title: Archery practice
cards.body: When practicing archery, you don't just judge yourself on a single arrow. You want to measure your overall accuracy across all your shots to truly improve.

cards.title: What is a cost function?
cards.body: A cost function (or loss function) is a formula that calculates a single score representing the model's total error across the entire dataset.

cards.title: Golf scores
cards.body: Think of a cost function like a golf score. Lower scores are better. Your ultimate goal is to get this cost score as close to zero as possible.

cards.title: Predicting home prices
cards.body: If your model predicts a house costs $300k but it sells for $350k, the error is $50k. The cost function combines all such errors into one overall score.

cards.title: Not just raw addition
cards.body: We don't just add up raw errors. If one guess is $10k too high and another is $10k too low, they would cancel out to zero, hiding the model's mistakes!

cards.title: Single error vs. cost
cards.body: The distinction between one error and the total score:

left.text: The distance from one data point to the prediction line.

right.text: The combined total of all errors across the entire dataset.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of a cost function in machine learning?

options.text: To measure the model's overall error and guide it to improve
To increase the size of the training dataset
To speed up the training process

exercises.explain: The cost function outputs a single score showing how wrong the model is. We minimize this score to improve the model.

exercises.prompt: True or false: We want the output of a cost function to be as close to zero as possible.
exercises.explain: True! A cost of zero means the model makes perfect predictions with no errors.

exercises.sentence: A cost function calculates a single ___ representing the model's total error across all data points.
exercises.explain: The cost function compresses all individual errors into a single numeric score.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a cost function at work?

options.text: A formula calculating the total distance between all house sales and predicted prices
A database storing the addresses and prices of homes
A chart showing the average home price by city

exercises.explain: A cost function measures error, such as the total distance between actual and predicted house prices.

exercises.prompt: You train two models to predict delivery times. Model A has a cost score of 15.2, and Model B has a cost score of 4.8. Which model should you deploy?

options.text: Model B, because a lower cost score means smaller overall errors
Model A, because a higher cost score means it learned more
Neither, because cost scores cannot compare different models

exercises.explain: Perfect! A lower cost score means the predictions are closer to the actual delivery times on average. Next: let's explore the most common way to calculate this score!
